module.name: ast-helpers
module.description: Reusable AST helper functions shared across multiple rule implementations.

scenarios.name: should return the declaration identifier name for a FunctionDeclaration
scenarios.given: a FunctionDeclaration node with an id identifier
scenarios.then: returns the function's declared name as a string

scenarios.name: should return null for a FunctionExpression
scenarios.given: a FunctionExpression node

scenarios.name: should return null for an ArrowFunctionExpression
scenarios.given: an ArrowFunctionExpression node

scenarios.name: should return the method key name when a function is inside a MethodDefinition
scenarios.given: a FunctionExpression whose parent is a MethodDefinition with an Identifier key

scenarios.name: should return null when the parent is not a MethodDefinition
scenarios.given: a FunctionDeclaration whose parent is a Program node

scenarios.name: should return the variable name when a function is the initializer of a VariableDeclarator
scenarios.given: an ArrowFunctionExpression whose parent is a VariableDeclarator

scenarios.name: should return null when the parent is not a VariableDeclarator
scenarios.given: a FunctionDeclaration with a Program parent

scenarios.name: should return the name of an Identifier node
scenarios.given: an Identifier AST node

scenarios.name: should return null for a non-Identifier node
scenarios.given: a Literal AST node

scenarios.name: should return null for null or undefined
scenarios.given: null or undefined

scenarios.name: should return the property name for dot-notation access
scenarios.given: a non-computed MemberExpression with an Identifier property

scenarios.name: should return the string value for computed string-literal access
scenarios.given: a computed MemberExpression with a string Literal property

scenarios.name: should return null for computed non-string access
scenarios.given: a computed MemberExpression with a non-string property

scenarios.name: should return name and replacement when the property is in the replacement map
scenarios.given: a MemberExpression whose property name exists in the replacement map
scenarios.then: returns an object with name and replacement fields

scenarios.name: should return null when the property is not in the replacement map
scenarios.given: a MemberExpression whose property name is absent from the map

scenarios.name: should return null when getMemberPropertyName returns null
scenarios.given: a computed MemberExpression with a non-string property

scenarios.name: should return the declaration name for a FunctionDeclaration
scenarios.given: a named FunctionDeclaration node

scenarios.name: should return the variable name for an arrow function assigned to a const
scenarios.given: an ArrowFunctionExpression whose parent is a VariableDeclarator

scenarios.name: should return the method name for a class method
scenarios.given: a FunctionExpression inside a MethodDefinition

scenarios.name: should return the anonymous sentinel when no name can be resolved
scenarios.given: an immediately-invoked arrow function with no reachable identifier

scenarios.name: should return the max property from a plain option object
scenarios.given: a plain object with a max property
scenarios.then: returns the value of the max property

scenarios.name: should return undefined when the option is not a plain object
scenarios.given: a non-object value such as a string or number

scenarios.name: should return undefined when the option is null or undefined
scenarios.given: null or undefined as the option

scenarios.name: should return filtered nodes when the value is an array containing node-like objects
scenarios.given: an array of mixed node-like and non-node-like values

scenarios.name: should return a single-element array when the value is a node-like object
scenarios.given: a single node-like object
scenarios.then: returns an array containing that node

scenarios.name: should return an empty array when the value is neither an array nor a node-like object
scenarios.given: a primitive value such as null or a string

scenarios.name: should return all direct child nodes using the source-code visitor keys
scenarios.given: an AST node and an ESLint SourceCode instance with visitor keys
scenarios.then: returns an array of all child nodes reachable via visitor keys

scenarios.name: should return an empty array when the node type has no visitor keys
scenarios.given: an AST node whose type has an empty visitor key list
